title: dipladenia polymorpha subvar. volubilis
intro: Dipladenia polymorpha subvar. volubilis, also known as Mandevilla, is a flowering plant native to South America, characterized by its evergreen vines and trumpet-shaped flowers. Traditionally, Mandevilla has been used in herbal medicine, particularly in Brazil and Argentina, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Mandevilla can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in folk medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
